name: ai-chat-header
title: AI Chat Header
description: Assistant identity, system-status badge, conversation scope, and new-chat/close controls.

props.name: subtitle
props.description: Conversation title or scope line, e.g. the patient and encounter in context.

props.name: status
props.description: System state only. `unavailable` is for surfaces where the assistant is switched off by policy.

props.name: statusLabel
props.description: Overrides the default label for the status.

props.name: onNewChat
props.description: Renders the new-conversation control when supplied.

props.name: onClose
props.description: Renders the close control when supplied.

clinicalNotes: Status describes the system, never the clinical quality of an answer: "Preparing response" is legitimate, "High confidence" is not — that would read as diagnostic certainty, which no chat header can honestly assert. Each status is carried by a shape-and-text badge as well as a coloured dot. Use `unavailable` where local policy switches the assistant off (a paediatric chart, a restricted record) and say why in the subtitle; a disabled assistant with no explanation invites workarounds.
notes: Status config is exported as aiChatHeaderStatuses if a product needs to mirror the labels elsewhere.
